title: Cyathea bullata
taxonomy: Cyathea bullata belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that produce spores instead of seeds. More specifically, it falls within the class Polypodiopsida and the order Cyatheales. This species is a member of the family Cyatheaceae, a group of tree ferns. Within the genus Cyathea, C. bullata is classified as a species found in New Zealand. It is a distinctive tree fern with unique characteristics, contributing to its classification within the plant kingdom.

In some cultures, the plant is used to treat skin conditions and wounds due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
